bestUseCase: Diversified BDC sleeve without picking one name.
mainRisks: BDC ETF acquired fund fees materially increase headline expense
name: VanEck BDC Income ETF
role: Broad BDC beta in one wrapper.
sourceLabel: VanEck BDC Income ETF — Summary Prospectus dated 2026-05-01 (SEC filing, link below). Fee table: management fee 0.40%, other expenses 0.02%, acquired fund fees and expenses 9.27%, total annual fund operating expenses 9.69%. The large acquired-fund line is mostly fees/expenses of the underlying BDCs held by the fund, not only the ETF wrapper fee. GhostYield leaves expenseRatio null because this row uses expenseRatio as a simple ETF net-expense-style field elsewhere; we are not loading the prospectus total (with acquired fund fees) into that field until semantics are explicit. Performance: average annual total return before taxes, periods ended 2025-12-31, one year -4.62% (stored as marketPerformance1Y); the excerpt used does not give separate NAV vs market columns or a three-year fund figure. Return-of-capital is discussed qualitatively in the prospectus only—no percentage keyed from a 19a notice.
yieldSource: Basket of BDC common equity dividends.

avoidIf: You cannot stomach middle-market credit marks, fee stack complexity, or shares trading at a steep premium to last NAV.
bestUseCase: Flagship externally managed BDC sleeve when you want scale, sponsor diversification, and filing-backed NII versus the regular quarterly dividend.
mainRisks: Middle-market credit: non-accruals 1.8% of total investments at amortized cost (1.2% at fair value) as of 12/31/25 per earnings exhibit
Debt/equity 1.12x (1.08x net of available cash definition in exhibit) — floating-rate borrowing costs and regulatory asset coverage
External manager (Ares affiliate): base management fee, income-based fee, and capital-gains incentive fee affect net earnings
Listed common can trade at premium or discount to last disclosed NAV; no closing stock price in this exhibit
name: Ares Capital Corp
role: Single-name flagship BDC exposure.
sourceLabel: Ares Capital Corporation — Form 8-K Exhibit 99.1 filed 2026-02-04 (SEC accession 000128775026000004), Q4 and year-ended 12/31/25 results plus dividend declaration. NAV per share $19.94 as of 12/31/25. Net investment income $0.52 per share in Q4'25 vs regular quarterly dividend $0.48 declared for the quarter (NII/regular dividend ~108% for the quarter). Board declared Q1'26 dividend $0.48 payable 2026-03-31 to holders of record 2026-03-13. latestDistributionAmount/date keyed to Q4'25 regular dividend paid 2025-12-30 (per exhibit). Debt/equity 1.12x as of 12/31/25. First lien senior secured loans 61% of portfolio at fair value; weighted average yield on accruing debt at fair value 10.3%. expenseRatio not keyed (BDC fee wording in filing is not reduced to a single ETF-style ratio here). No closing NYSE price in exhibit — marketPrice, premiumDiscount, and market-based currentYield left null.
yieldSource: BDC dividends from middle-market lending.

avoidIf: You want minimal leverage and zero complexity from incentive fees, or you dislike Blackstone-affiliate management alignment questions.
bestUseCase: Large senior-secured, first-lien–tilted BDC when you care about filing-disclosed NII coverage of the regular dividend and sponsor-scale origination.
mainRisks: Credit and sponsor risk despite 97.6% first-lien at fair value (12/31/25) — loans are private and can mark down in stress
Ending debt-to-equity 1.30x and average 1.27x in Q4’25 — interest expense path matters
Non-accruals 0.6% of portfolio at amortized cost (0.5% at fair value) per Q4’25 materials
External manager (Blackstone Private Credit Strategies LLC): management and income-based incentive fees
Shares can trade away from last NAV; distributionRate here is the issuer's dividend yield on NAV (per company definition), not a live market yield
name: Blackstone Secured Lending
role: Another large BDC print — overlap watch with ARCC peers.
sourceLabel: Blackstone Secured Lending Fund — Form 8-K Exhibit 99.1 dated 2026-02-25 (SEC accession 000121390026020083). **JSON distributionRate 0.114 (11.4%)** = issuer-defined dividend yield on **NAV per share** only: annualized 4Q'25 regular dividend divided by **12/31/25 NAV/sh $26.92** — **not** market-price current yield (no closing stock price in this exhibit). NAV $6.2B as of 12/31/25. Q4'25 net investment income $0.80 vs regular dividend $0.77 (issuer coverage 104%). Board declared Q1'26 dividend $0.77: record 2026-03-31, payable ~2026-04-24 — latestDistributionDate uses that stated payment date. Ending debt-to-equity 1.30x; weighted average yield on performing debt at fair value 9.6%; ~97.6% first lien; non-accruals 0.6% at cost. Externally managed; expenseRatio not reduced to a single comparable ratio here. marketPrice, premiumDiscount, currentYield null.
yieldSource: BDC lending and fee-related economics — dividend forward.
